A decade ago, a teddy bear randomly drawn from an arcade claw machine foretold Tarris Reed's future as a Spurs' draft pick.

That cosmic connection comes to fruition as Reed makes his Summer League debut today:

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs... via @expressnews

media.title: Why San Antonio first-round pick Tarris Reed Jr. was meant to be a Spur
media.description: After a chance claw machine victory as a child gave the former UConn big man a connection with the Spurs, he's ready to put on the black and silver in the Summer League.

items.title: Breaking: The New York Knicks and free agent center Andre Drummond have agreed to a one-year, $3.9 million deal, league sources told HoopsHype. During his career, Drummond has been a two-time All-Star and a four-time rebounding champion.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Breaking: The New York Knicks and free agent center Andre Drummond have agreed to a one-year, $3.9 million deal, league sources told HoopsHype. During his career, Drummond has been a two-time All-Star and a four-time rebounding champion.

items.title: Players who sign a one-year contract, or a two-year contract with a player or team option on Year 2, and will have Bird or Early Bird rights, get an implied no-trade clause. Players can choose to waive this implied NTC upon signing their contract.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt: Players who sign a one-year contract, or a two-year contract with a player or team option on Year 2, and will have Bird or Early Bird rights, get an implied no-trade clause. Players can choose to waive this implied NTC upon signing their contract.

items.title: The Deandre Ayton trade further proves one of the least credited rules in the NBA: That every Lakers big man eventually has to play for the Wizards and vice versa.
items.author: Fred Katz
items.body_excerpt:
The Deandre Ayton trade further proves one of the least credited rules in the NBA: That every Lakers big man eventually has to play for the Wizards and vice versa. 

Ayton, AD, Dwight, Markieff Morris, Wagner, JaVale, Thomas Bryant — the cycle will never end.
